item.content:encoded:
As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy…

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-07-18 09:50 UTC

OP

As I was saying yesterday. The Chinese are slowly giving the news to trading partners. No more dollars. You want to buy from us. You get a Yuan swap line. You settle trade imbalances in gold or Yuan and use our settlement systems.

What can the US do? All they have is threats against the likes of Argentina. China has the vital goods they need at the right prices. The will continue to do this and kill dollar dominance.

Theres no other choice or enter an inflationary spiral.

62495806
You own the YUAN?
Communism out here selling dreams like a busted dude promising everybody a mansion while he stealing the fridge out the kitchen. Every single time they talk about “everybody equal,” somehow the regular people end up broke, hungry, standing in lines for bread while the top bosses chilling fat behind locked gates acting like kings.
They be saying, “share everything,” but what it really mean is: “you own nothing, the government own YOU.” Wanna speak your mind? Nah. Wanna worship freely? Nah. Wanna disagree? Congratulations, now you suspicious. Whole system move like a control freak ex that gotta monitor every little thing you do.
And the economy? Absolute clown show. Folks working hard just to get empty shelves and busted apartments while propaganda posters smiling harder than the actual citizens. History got receipts too — censorship, secret police, people disappearing, leaders acting untouchable while preaching “for the people” every five minutes.
That’s the wild part about communism: it start sounding like a superhero speech and end looking like a prison with extra paperwork. They promise equality, then everybody equally miserable except the dudes running the show.
At some point you gotta ask: if the system so perfect, why people always trying to escape it instead of rushing in?

>>62495971
You don't have to live in America to use the dollar. Why would you have to live in China to use the Yuan?

62496252
>>62496108
No kidding but the US’s status as the world reserve currency is pretty unique; it wasn’t exactly an organic happening. Going from a gold-backed reserve currency to a fiat reserve currency has a lot of benefits, no doubt, especially since US debt is denominated in dollars. Still, China isn’t even close to having a freely floating currency, and their dominance on the global stage still comes from their export advantage. I’m not saying that they don’t want the position, I’m just saying it’s probably premature to claim they’re going for it.

item.title: The older I get, the more I realize that NEETs are right about everything. — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The older I get, the more I realize that NEETs are right about everything.

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-07-18 10:23 UTC

OP

The older I get, the more I realize that NEETs are right about everything. I was a reasonably successful programmer who got laid off and I haven't been able to find work in almost a year. My family has been burning through savings like crazy. Meanwhile my brother is a NEET who seems to have experienced 0.1% of the hardship I've gone through. He's always cheerful and spends most of his time socializing or playing video games. It feels like he somehow perceived the stakes of the modern world correctly, while I fucked up by following the standard cultural script. NEET really is the correct move on the game board if you can pull it off and if you're able to see it. Society must have completely unraveled for this to be true, but it is.

Early replies (first 3)

62496918
Demoralization thread

Arbeit macht frei

62496920
>OP is a faggot and will never be a woman

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK? — 115 replies — 11.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why is there so much negativity surrounding the UK?

Open thread • Replies: 115 • Updated: 2026-07-17 23:57 UTC

OP

Every time I see the UK mentioned here, it is always in a negative light.

It is always
>In the USA, wages are better!
>In Europe, houses are cheaper!
>The FTSE is underperforming!
>My taxes are too high!

There is never any mention of the fantastic benefits which come with life in the UK. The fantastic healthcare for minimal cost, the great education system available to everybody, the dynamic modern economy with many bustling sectors, and everything else.

Taxes are higher than the USA but they are lower than most of Europe and we still enjoy fantastic public services whereas in America there are no public services or safety nets.

Not to mention the things you cannot measure. Being able to walk the streets in safety without fear of gun violence and gangs. Enjoying the fantastic beautiful nature and gardens. Living in the historical majesty and balancing that with modern cosmopolitan qualities.

The UK might not be the best place to become rich but nobody is poor here and it is very easy to enjoy life if you have goals beyond the number in your bank account.

Early replies (first 3)

62481973
>>62481958
the problem is that its being filled with immigrants who are living off tax payers money
thats the gist of why everyone is so pissed off here

62481986
>>62481973
So just right wing racebaiting?

No, I think it runs deeper than that. Most people have not personally had negative experiences with migrants, and reading the Daily Mail does not count.

I am trying to angle from a financial perspective too. People talk about the UK like it is a poor country but it is one of the richest countries on the planet. I think perhaps they are just spoiled and do not know what poor countries are really like. I blame social media.

62481994
>>62481958
>fantastic healthcare for minimal cost
my nan lived in birmingham and she came down with leukaemia and was gone within a couple of months. She never even got to see a doctor because the muslim immigrants were getting seen first. if i had something to lose i wouldnt even dare post this for fear of legal reprimand.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62490030
>>62487539

OP

I give up. It's over. With the news that zcash will eventually eliminate the transparent pool (see link below) it has utterly defeated the last argument that Monero ever had against it.

The argument against optional privacy was a pretty good one. Privacy shouldn't be something you have to opt into. Now zcash has patched up even that argument.

So what do you have? You have an extremely well funded coin with mandatory privacy, on all exchanges, with all the marketing and engineering budget to expand forever. Jews in support. Institutions coming. MSTR holding companies. Price skyrocketing. KOLs. Lobbyists. And the ONLY arguments against it are spurious and hard to prove. "Muh it has a backdoor" (can't be demonstrated) "Muh jews" (nobody cares) "Muh VCs" (nobody cares).

In comparison Monero is entirely self funded. Barely enough budget to keep its ~10 devs employed. No marketing at all. The only "success" it has is being used for acquiring fucking drugs on a couple of shady marketplaces nobody has ever heard of.

I loved what Monero stood for and I thought that investing in privacy would be a life changing financial decision. What actually happened was Jews stole your momentum, used it for themselves, adopted ALL of your tech and design decisions, ALL of your memes, and enriched and empowered themselves. Meanwhile you pretend your bazaar or drug market makes you superior. You, the loser goy, are destined to this life of serfdom and hardship. I thought I was amongst people who understood the reality of power. Apparently not.

https://x.com/Zcash/status/2077150354505118014

>$581 ZEC vs $332 XMR and climbing

Outside of permissionless p2p payments, blockchains are a solution in search of a problem.

The mainstream white market thus simply has no need for blockchains in general and privacy coins in particular. Nigerian ZEC baggies desperately push the white market adoption meme because ZEC ia profoundly toxic brand in black and grey markets where Monero has already established itself as a virtual monopoly. 62494781
>>62494291
We get this same story every other month now. Everyone thought it was over when DeepSeek came out and then it turned out to not really matter that much. Now Chinese media keeps trying to desperately recreate the Deekseek shock when they just don't have the same shock value anymore.
Chinese models will never matter because local models don't really matter. You either have to use an extremely compressed model that can run on retail hardware but is shit, or you can spend ludicrous amounts of money on a dedicated AI server that you are pretty much guaranteed to never make your money back on.
The one good thing to come out of the Chinese open source models is that they scare the living bajeezus out of American AI companies and force them to offer better, more price competitive products. Most American AI companies even release their own open source models now. Llama in particular is very popular these days.
